title: silene nocturna f. brachypetala
intro: Silene nocturna f. brachypetala, also known as evening primrose, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family and is native to Europe. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds which are rich in gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an essential fatty acid with anti-inflammatory and moisturizing effects. The health benefits of Silene nocturna f. brachypetala include relief from symptoms associated with premenstrual syndrome, menopause, and skin conditions such as eczema, as well as improved cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant include oil and capsules, which can be taken orally or applied topically to the skin for therapeutic benefits.
